clues: First observed in the late 19th and early 20th century, this life-threatening disease is always secondary to an underlying condition
Due to its high mortality, it has sometimes been grimly referred to as 'death is coming'
Also known as a consumption coagulopathy, early researchers identified this as a rapid widespread clot formation exhausting the body's clotting factors
Because of the unprecedented scale of trench warfare and subsequent wounded and disabled soldiers from WWI, the first formal school of this specialty was created at Walter Reed Army Hospital
Initially a field founded and developed primarily by women, women continue to make up approximately 70% of the profession
Although early practitioners were known as 'reconstruction aides', the field has expanded to help with chronic pain, pelvic floor health, and vestibular issues
Do you know the original name for 7-Up? Discovered in 1817 in Sweden, this was an original ingredient in 7-Up until it was banned in the 1940s

Think back to high school chemistry class. A key component of red fireworks because of how it burns, modern medical uses of this medication were based on a misconception that it would treat excess uric acid in the brain
This substance is an alkali metal and famously can cause nephrogenic diabetes insipidus, affecting 10-40% of patients when used long term
This disease was first described by an otolaryngologist in 1897 who identified rapid destruction of the nose and face
While lungs and kidneys are classic sites affected by small clusters of immune cells, almost any part of the body can be involved
In 2010, this disease was officially renamed due to historical ties of the prior eponymous discoverer with the Nazi movement

Documented for over 4,000 years, Mesopotamians viewed this condition as a spiritual curse. Hippocrates was among the first to describe this condition without relying on supernatural explanations, instead suggesting it originated in the brain
Historical treatments included remedies like covering the affected organ with warm bread to 'sweat out' the affliction or even surgeries drilling into nearby bone aimed to release 'trapped air'
Make sure to use proper ear protection. Loud noise exposure is a common cause
